task: Built and deployed a four-stage permission gate system to prevent dangerous task injection in unrestricted sessions, added task approval workflow with notification integration, and completed Phase 3.5 security hardening with full test coverage and roadmap updates.
discussion_summary: This session completed the permission gate feature (Phases 3.5 guard) by implementing four sequential stages: dangerous-permission detection in permission_gate.py, task queue hold logic in session_start.py, approval/discard UI in the Cursor extension, and comprehensive test coverage. The system now blocks auto-injection of queued tasks when sessions have skip-permissions or unrestricted Bash/rm commands in allow lists, requiring explicit user approval before proceeding. All 13 new tests pass; roadmap and goals backlog updated to reflect Phase 3.5 completion.

accomplishments.what: LinkedIn location combobox field filling fixed with city name extraction and fallback retry pattern
Identified root cause of LinkedIn location field failures: full location strings do not trigger city autocomplete dropdown
Implemented two-part fix: prompt instructs extraction of city name from full location string, with retry on failure
Killed orphaned uvicorn process blocking backend logs
Conducted comprehensive security audit of apply agent code generation paths with four hardening fixes against prompt injection attacks
Fixed resume PDF portfolio URL lookup from qa_bank.portfolio_url to application_prefill.answers.portfolio_url
Updated PDF generator to render 'Portfolio' as link label instead of domain URL
Diagnosed Ramp application failure as Ashby spam_warning state (browser fingerprinting-based anti-bot detection)
Implemented spam_warning recovery with 'Learn more' probe to locate submit/submit-again button
Extended spam_warning handling to distinguish overlay banner (resubmit after scroll) vs form replacement (hard refresh required)
Refactored spam recovery strategy to defer spam-flagged jobs to end of session instead of inline retry
Investigated queue drain architecture and browser_stream replay buffer lifecycle
Implemented permission_gate.py to detect dangerous permissions (skip-permissions, unrestricted Bash, rm in allow list)
Implemented session_start.py to hold queued tasks instead of auto-injecting when dangerous permissions detected
Wired task_approval_pending notification type into Cursor extension.js for IDE popup rendering
Added test_permission_gate.py with 13 passing tests covering all dangerous permission detection scenarios
Added test_task_approval_gate.py with full coverage for approval gate workflow
Updated roadmap.md to reflect Phase 3.5 (guard) completion and Phase 4 (approval workflow) status
Auto-completed 'dangerously-skip-permissions sessions before queued' goal via Stop-hook inference

in_progress.what: Architectural design for stateful retry mechanism that captures failure context (screenshots, error reasoning) to enable learning-based job resubmission instead of blind retry

next_actions.action: Cut real git tag + GitHub release so Formula sha256/url are real, and create homebrew-askr tap repo
next_actions.why: Unblocks Formula installation and public distribution; currently using placeholder URLs

next_actions.action: Extend pre_tool_use.py cross-repo boundary check to cover Bash tool calls, not just Write/Edit/MultiEdit
next_actions.why: Security gap: Bash commands can read/write files outside repo root without guard enforcement

next_actions.action: Fix PreCompact emergency handover to route through real LLM handover path instead of hardcoded boilerplate
next_actions.why: checkpoint.py create_checkpoint trigger_type==emergency branch currently uses static text; should use full handover generation

next_actions.action: Audit whether guard_warning notification type actually renders in Cursor extension, since it is not in the extension type whitelist
next_actions.why: Phase 3.5 guard_warning notifications may be silently dropped; need to verify rendering or add to whitelist

next_actions.action: Update README.md to reflect Phase 3 (notifications) and Phase 3.5 (guard) as completed and running, not 'Coming Next'
next_actions.why: Documentation is stale; both phases are fully implemented and deployed

decisions.decision: Hold queued tasks in dangerous sessions instead of auto-injecting; require explicit user approval via task_approval_pending notification
decisions.reason: Prevents accidental execution of dangerous commands (skip-permissions, unrestricted Bash/rm) that could harm system or data; gives user control over risky operations

decisions.decision: Detect dangerous permissions at session_start time, not at task injection time
decisions.reason: Catches risk early in session lifecycle; allows user to adjust permissions before any tasks are queued

decisions.decision: Defer spam-flagged Ashby jobs to end of session instead of inline retry
decisions.reason: Prevents cascade abort from repeated spam detection; improves application throughput by batching recovery attempts

decisions.decision: Distinguish two Ashby spam_warning cases: overlay banner (scroll + resubmit) vs form replacement (hard refresh + re-fill)
decisions.reason: Ashby anti-bot detection uses different UI patterns; single recovery strategy was insufficient

failed_approaches.approach: Inline retry of spam-flagged jobs during active application session
failed_approaches.reason: Triggered cascade abort when Ashby spam detection persisted; browser fingerprinting-based detection requires session reset

failed_approaches.approach: Using full location string for LinkedIn location combobox field
failed_approaches.reason: Full location strings do not trigger city autocomplete dropdown; city name extraction required

relational_files.why: permission_gate.py and session_start.py use writer.py for state persistence and decision logging
Append-only log of all commands and decisions; tracks session lifecycle and goal completion
Cursor extension UI renders task_approval_pending notifications from permission gate workflow
Goals backlog tracks Phase 3.5 completion and remaining work items

completed_goals: Add test coverage for pre_tool_use.py/guard_runner.py - the implementation guard has zero tests despite being the security-critical path
